messages.content: On 6/21/24, a mother entered to pharmacy to pick up this medication for her son in which she asked the question whether this would be safe for her son to take if while he was experiencing damage to his liver.

The pharmacist advised the mother to consult with her son's healthcare provider before administering the medication, as liver damage can affect the way the body processes medications. The pharmacist also recommended that the mother inform the healthcare provider about any other medications or supplements her son may be taking, as they could also interact with the medication in question. The pharmacist emphasized the importance of proper communication with healthcare providers to ensure safe and effective treatment.

reword: On 6/21/24, a mother entered to pharmacy to pick up this medication for her son in which she asked the question whether this would be safe for her son to take if while he was experiencing damage to his liver.
On June 21, 2024, a concerned mother visited the pharmacy to collect medication for her son and inquired about the safety of the medication if her son had liver damage.

After further research, it was found that fenasteride is metabolized in the liver. Therefore, further guidlines had to be reviewed in order to note whether the drug is not an appropraite option for her son or if a dose adjustment was needed.

Upon conducting additional research, it was discovered that fenasteride is metabolized in the liver. As a result, the pharmacist needed to review further guidelines to determine if the medication was contraindicated for the mother's son or if a dose adjustment was necessary.
As a result, we needed to review further guidelines to determine if the medication was contraindicated for the mother’s son or if a dose adjustment was necessary.

We had to review additional guidelines to assess if the medication was not recommended for the mother's son due to his liver damage or if a dose adjustment was required to ensure safe and effective treatment.
Individuals with hepatic disease will have a harder time metabolizing/ridding their body of the drug causing more serious side effects and increased plasma drug levels.

People with liver disease may experience difficulties in metabolizing and eliminating the medication from their body, which can lead to more severe side effects and elevated levels of the drug in their bloodstream.

The mother was advised to not pick up the medication until her son's hepatic levels had improved. The mother was also advised to call the patient's doctor so that they are aware of the situation. An alternative recommendation was not offered to the mother at this time.

The pharmacist recommended that the mother refrain from picking up the medication until her son's hepatic levels had improved. In addition, the mother was advised to inform the patient's doctor of the situation. However, no alternative medication recommendation was provided to the mother at that time.

write a reflection describing the pharmacy practice setting that I was assigned including - my preceptors primary role and function, her positive influences that i observed, the physical setting and types of activities/services that occur regularly in the practice setting at cvs retail pharmacy. What barriers are there to providing these services

During my assignment at the CVS retail pharmacy, I had the opportunity to work with my preceptor, who served as the pharmacist-in-charge of the practice setting. One of her primary roles and functions was to ensure that the pharmacy was operating efficiently and effectively, with a keen focus on patient safety and satisfaction. She regularly reviewed new prescription orders, dispensed medications, provided patient counseling, and collaborated with other healthcare providers to optimize patient care.

Throughout my experience, I observed several positive influences that my preceptor demonstrated, including her excellent communication skills, compassionate care towards patients, and meticulous attention to detail. Additionally, she demonstrated a strong commitment to continuing education, constantly seeking new ways to improve patient outcomes by staying up-to-date with the latest research and advancements in pharmacy practice. Her dedication to the profession was truly inspiring and I appreciated the opportunity to learn from her.

The physical setting of the CVS retail pharmacy was compact but efficient, with well-organized shelves and a computerized system for filling and dispensing prescriptions. Services that occurred regularly in the practice setting included providing medication delivery and refills, administering immunizations, conducting medication reviews, and counseling patients on medication usage and side effects. The pharmacy was constantly busy, with several patients coming in and out at all times of the day.

Despite the positive aspects of the retail pharmacy setting, there were still certain barriers that could limit the provision of these services. One of the main barriers was the lack of staffing, which made it challenging for the pharmacy to provide certain services, such as conducting immunizations or medication therapy management. Additionally, high patient volume and insurance restrictions sometimes limited the ability of pharmacists to fully engage with patients and provide personalized care.

Overall, my experience at the CVS retail pharmacy provided me with a deeper understanding of the pharmacy practice setting and the role of pharmacists in patient care. I was inspired by the dedication and professionalism of my preceptor and hopeful that, despite existing barriers, the pharmacy can continue to provide high-quality care to their patients.

Throughout my first IPPE rotation, I had the privilege of learning from my preceptor Dr. Vanessa Assemi, the supervising pharmacist of CVS in West Babylon, NY. Vanessa shared her career path with me as she has been in the pharmaceutical industry for over 17 years. Vanessa received her undergraduate degree for Stony Brook University where she studied health sciences. Vanessa was inspired to go to pharmacy school after 8 years of being a technician at CVS. She received her PharmD degree through a two year accelerated programs from the University of St. Joseph in West Hartford, Connecticut. As an intern with no prior pharmacy experience, I am grateful for the opportunity to learn from Vanessa and her knowledgeable staff.

During my IPPE rotation, I had the pleasure of working with my preceptor, Dr. Vanessa Assemi, the supervising pharmacist of CVS in West Babylon, NY. Vanessa was a fantastic mentor who generously shared her wealth of knowledge and experience with me.

Vanessa has had an impressive career in the pharmaceutical industry, spanning over 17 years. She started her career as a technician at CVS and was inspired to become a pharmacist after eight years in that role. After earning her undergraduate degree in Health Sciences from Stony Brook University, Vanessa pursued a PharmD degree through a two-year accelerated program at the University of St. Joseph in West Hartford, Connecticut.

As an intern with no prior pharmacy experience, I was fortunate to learn from Vanessa and her team of knowledgeable staff members. During my rotation, I was exposed to a variety of pharmacy services, including medication dispensing, immunizations, and patient counseling. Vanessa's passion for pharmacy and commitment to patient care was inspiring, and that was reflected in her team’s attitude towards patient care.

Overall, my experience at the CVS retail pharmacy in West Babylon, NY was rewarding and insightful. Vanessa and her team showed me how rewarding it can be to work in pharmacy and helped me develop valuable skills for my future career. I am grateful for the opportunity to work with such experienced and passionate healthcare professionals.

Vanessa possesses many skills such as being personable with patients, going above and beyond to help her community with medication costs, being organized, thorough/detail oriented, and caring towards her staff.

Vanessa is an accomplished pharmacist who possesses a diverse skill set that makes her stand out within the industry. Among her many skills, Vanessa is highly personable and always goes above and beyond to help patients in need. She has a deep commitment to her community and has worked tirelessly to help patients with medication costs.

Vanessa is also an incredibly organized and detail-oriented pharmacist, with an innate ability to keep track of prescription orders and ensure that medication is dispensed accurately and efficiently. She understands the importance of maintaining accurate records and takes every opportunity to provide high-quality care to her patients.

One of Vanessa's most notable qualities is her compassion and care towards her staff. She recognizes the importance of fostering a positive and supportive work environment, and she always ensures that her team feels valued, respected, and appreciated. Her willingness to listen and collaborate with her team is a testament to her exceptional leadership skills.

Overall, Vanessa is an outstanding pharmacist who embodies everything that the profession stands for. She is committed to providing high-quality care to her patients and always puts their needs first. Her diverse skill set and exceptional leadership qualities make her an invaluable asset within the industry and a role model to aspiring healthcare professionals.
what barriers are there to providing pharmacy services

Pharmacies face various barriers when providing healthcare services. 

One of the main barriers is the lack of adequate staffing. Due to understaffing, pharmacists sometimes cannot devote enough time to providing high-quality services to their patients, leading to significant burnout. Secondly, certain insurance restrictions limit the provision of services such as medication therapy management, or counseling patients. Pharmacies may also face challenges while servicing patients in geographical locations. Some rural or remote communities may not have easy access to pharmacy services, limiting their access to medication and counseling.

Another significant issue is the increasing workload, which can impact the effectiveness of pharmacists in providing patient care. Apart from dispensing medication, pharmacists must manage a variety of tasks such as insurance claims, paperwork, administration, and inventory management, which may leave insufficient time to offer personalized care to each patient. 

Finally, the rapid growth in prescription medications and their relatively high costs may represent another barrier for patients in obtaining the medications they need. Due to high drug costs or lack of insurance, patients may not be able to afford the medications they require, leading to a significant barrier in access to healthcare services. 

Overall, pharmacies face various challenges that can impede their ability to provide high-quality services to patients. Addressing these obstacles requires collaboration between healthcare stakeholders, the industry, and policymakers to create effective and impactful healthcare policies and solutions.

reflect on the physical setting and types of activities/services that occur regularly in the practice setting - how well matched are the services of the practice to the needs of the patient population and what barriers are there to providing these services?

During my IPPE rotation at CVS retail pharmacy, the physical setting was well-organized, and the team was efficiently managing prescription orders, dispensing medications, and carrying out other patient-centered services. The pharmacy had a good range of services, including vaccinations, medication reviews, and counseling to enhance the well-being of the patients who visited the pharmacy.

In terms of matching services to the needs of the patient population, the pharmacy provided services that catered to the patients' requirements effectively. For instance, the pharmacy offered a wide range of medications, from over-the-counter to prescription medicines, which provided the patient population with a variety of options. In addition, the pharmacy team would frequently follow up with patients taking chronic medications to ensure they were adhering to their medication regimen. 

However, barriers still existed in providing these services to patients. One of the primary challenges was inadequate staffing. The pharmacy was sometimes understaffed, which made it challenging to provide the quality of service required for each patient. In addition, insurance restrictions limit the provision of certain services. Some patients' insurance may not cover therapies, medications, or counseling sessions that the pharmacy team would typically recommend. 

Finally, certain groups of patients may face barriers to obtaining prescription medications due to their high costs. With increasing medication prices, some patients may have difficulty affording their medications, making it harder to adhere to their medication regimen or get the necessary medication refills, which poses significant barriers to providing optimal healthcare services.

Overall, the CVS retail pharmacy effectively provided patient-centered services that met the needs of the patient population. However, barriers such as staffing shortages, insurance restrictions, and high medication prices, still existed. Addressing these challenges requires collaboration between the healthcare industry, policymakers, and other healthcare stakeholders to come up with effective solutions that promote equitable, affordable, and efficient access to healthcare services.

My outlook on community pharmacy has positively changed and I look forward to having more opportunities in this type of setting. I could not have picked a better rotation site for me to have my first pharmacy experience.

Working with a diverse group of patients, as you mentioned, provides an opportunity to understand different perspectives, backgrounds, and beliefs related to healthcare. It can help in recognizing the barriers faced by different groups and creates room for implementing patient-centered care. A pharmacy practitioner who can effectively communicate and collaborate with a broad range of patients tends to provide better care to the population they serve. reword: 
In recent years, the pharmaceutical industry has faced many challenges that have limited their ability to provide optimal patient care in a community setting. These include medication shortages, high medication costs, complex medication regimens, and the privacy of patients/pharmacy staff. It is crucial for pharmacists to actively address these challenges in an industry that is constantly evolving. 
	Complex medication regimens has been the focus of discussion between my preceptor and I as we have encountered many patients dealing with an issue of some sort surrounding this area. CVS has attempted to address this issue by providing print out medication schedules for each patient. However, they are often overlooked by the patients or pharmacy staff which is not helping the situation. 
Retail pharmacy organization that have attempted to manage complex regimens or medication therapy management (MTM) include Costco, Walmart, and Walgreens which prompted our interest of how CVS will address this situation. Likewise, insurance plans such as Medicare drug coverage are required to offer MTM services to their patients. MTM is an important responsibility of a pharmacist to ensure that their patients understand their medications. These services may even help address medication errors, improve adherence, and create more effective medication use.

Throughout my rotation at CVS, the new thing there is to educate patients about the CVS app where they can access their records, refills, prescription status, and more. The goal of CVS is to get 80% of their patients to use and download the app for their convince. As a result, I would like to implement MTM services into the app under the pharmacy tab. As shown below using medication scheduling as an example, patients will be able to access precautions, schedules, and educational information about their prescriptions through their app. They will also have the ability to chat with a remote pharmacist about any questions they may have. This will not only help patients by allowing pharmacists to be more accessible, but also help with freeing up the workload of pharmacists who are in person. 

The target market for this would theoretically be all CVS patients. However, it is understandable that some individuals may have trouble with new technology. Therefore, it is projected that this idea would be more aimed toward a younger generation. I believe this will be a helpful tool for this population because people will have the ability to have their questions answered at their leisure. This will also help decrease overworked pharmacists who are in person by lessening their incoming calls. 

It is estimated that the development of this app would take at least 12 months to be fully functioning. The main resource in this case would be money, as it would be expensive to implement new technology into the app. CVS would also have to employ remote pharmacists and IT personnel. However, patients can pay a monthly fee of $14.99 for these services which may help supplement any outstanding costs. 

The success of this plan will be evaluated by tracking the metrics on how many individuals are utilizing this service through the app. This may also be measured by analyzing medication errors, as this should cause a decrease in errors. 

Three barriers of this idea include, patient trust that an online app will provide the same or better services that can be provided in person, hiring more employees in a field that is already so saturated, and 


This innovative plan does have the potential of being replicated by other pharmacies and organizations. 


After further discussion with my preceptor and pharmacy staff about this idea – there were mixed ideas as some people are for remote working and others are not. I ensured my site that this service would not be replacing in person pharmacists, but rather being an addition. My preceptor also noted that it would be beneficial for patients to access a variety of MTM services through the app and live pharmacist chat box. We believe this idea would help increase the health knowledge of more patients. We also belive this may provide a more personal experience for each individual as many of the patients who visit the pharmacy do not interact with the staff. We hope that this would provide a more personalized, accessible, option for providing optimal patient care

The pharmaceutical industry faces several challenges that limit their ability to provide optimal patient care in a community setting, including medication shortages, high medication costs, complex medication regimens, and privacy concerns. It's essential for pharmacists to actively address these challenges in an industry that is continuously evolving.

During my rotation, my preceptor and I discussed complex medication regimens, as we noted that many patients face issues in this area. While CVS has attempted to address this issue by providing printouts of medication schedules for each patient, they are often overlooked by patients and pharmacy staff, adding to the problem. Other retail pharmacy organizations, such as Costco, Walmart, and Walgreens, have attempted to manage complex medication regimens or medication therapy management (MTM). Insurance plans, such as Medicare drug coverage, are required to offer MTM services to their patients. Pharmacist involvement in MTM services is crucial to ensuring that patients understand their medications. These services may also help address medication errors, improve adherence, and create more effective medication use.
